title: MD
biography: Oliver Sacks was a British-American neurologist and author who brought neuroscience to the public through bestselling books including 'Awakenings' and 'The Man Who Mistook His Wife for a Hat.' He pioneered L-DOPA treatment for post-encephalitic patients and became one of the most celebrated science writers of the 20th century.
aiSummary: Oliver Sacks was a neurology specialist with an H-index of 45 at Columbia University and NYU. Renowned neurologist-author who brought neuroscience to the public through bestselling books. Based in New York, United States.

timeline.title: Began working with post-encephalitic patients
timeline.description: Started treating survivors of the 1920s encephalitis lethargica epidemic at Beth Israel Hospital

timeline.title: Published 'Awakenings'
timeline.description: Documented the dramatic effects of L-DOPA on post-encephalitic patients; later adapted into a film

timeline.title: Published 'The Man Who Mistook His Wife for a Hat'
timeline.description: Bestselling collection of neurological case studies that reached millions of readers

timeline.title: Appointed Professor of Neurology at Columbia
timeline.description: Joined Columbia University Medical Center as professor of neurology and psychiatry
